title: caltha introloba var. obtusa
intro: Caltha introloba var. obtusa, also known as Marsh Marigold or Buttercup, is a flowering plant belonging to the family Ranunculaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic properties, which have been utilized to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Caltha introloba var. obtusa include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin conditions, and act as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
